name: Sourdough Rye (with Caraway Seeds)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Rye Sourdough Starter, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the yeast, warm water, and sugar in a large bowl. Let stand until very foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. Dissolve the salt in the tepid water. Stir the mixture into the sourdough starter. Beat in the yeast mixture, then the caraway seeds (or substitute). Add the rye flour, about 1/2 cup at a time, then beat in the gluten flour until incorporated.
3. Spread 2 cups of all-purpose flour on a kneading surface and turn the dough onto it. Mix with a dough scraper or pancake turner, then knead just until thoroughly mixed, adding more rye flour as needed to form a medium-stiff dough, not too heavy.
4. Scrape the kneading surface, dusting it lightly with rye flour. Knead the dough thoroughly until elastic and smooth, without making it too heavy or overly floury.
5. Form the dough into a ball and place in an ungreased bowl (it will stick slightly, so avoid using metal); cover with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled in bulk, at least 1 hour.
6. Turn the dough onto the kneading surface, dusted lightly with rye flour. Remove any air bubbles by pressing, then shape into two smooth balls. Cover with a towel and let rest for 20 minutes while sprinkling cornmeal on a baking sheet.
